args.query: Stanford Health Care billing notice patient billing payment options financial assistance official

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Billing - Pay My Bill | Stanford\
  \ Health Care
- url: https://stanfordhealthcare.org/for-patients-visitors/billing.html
\
  - source: Stanford Health Care
- domain: stanfordhealthcare.org

## Content
\
  
# Billing

## Pay Your Bill Online

Stanford Health Care provides online billing\
  \ options through MyHealth. Patients can log in to a MyHealth account to see and\
  \ pay bills. The page also provides a Pay as Guest option through Stanford Health\
  \ Care's MyChart billing portal.

Patients who prefer phone payment can contact\
  \ the Patient Billing Customer Service Office at 1-800-549-3720. The listed business\
  \ hours are Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., with a daily lunch closure\
  \ from 11:30 a.m. to 12:30 p.m.

For mail payment, Stanford Health Care lists\
  \ the mailing address as:

Stanford Health Care  
P.O. Box 740715  
Los Angeles,\
  \ CA 90074-0715

## Shared Access and Billing Information

The billing page\
  \ explains that a MyHealth proxy account can be requested to view a child’\
  s or adult patient’s online health record and billing information. For care\
  \ received at Stanford Children’s Health, the page directs patients to the\
  \ Stanford Children’s Health MyChart program for child medical records and\
  \ medical bills.

## Payment Plans and Financial Assistance

Stanford Health\
  \ Care states that if a patient needs help paying a medical bill, payment plans\
  \ and financial assistance are available for people who cannot pay their balance\
  \ in full. The page links to financial assistance information for details.

##\
  \ Financial Counseling

Financial counseling is available to help patients navigate\
  \ the financial part of getting care at Stanford Health Care. Financial counselors\
  \ may explain insurance coverage and benefits, estimate financial responsibility\
  \ for services not covered by insurance, identify possible ways to pay for care\
  \ when funds or insurance are insufficient, help identify coverage through government\
  \ programs where available, and address questions about insurance coverage and financial\
  \ assistance.

Patients may reach a financial counselor at 844-498-2900, Monday\
  \ through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.

## Patient Refunds

Stanford Health\
  \ Care explains that patient refunds may be handled through a secure payment platform.\
  \ Patients can choose a preferred method for receiving a refund when applicable.\
  \ Payments made by credit card are refunded to the original form of payment when\
  \ possible. If a different payment form was used or the card is no longer available,\
  \ refunds may be issued through a secure portal or by check.
"
metadata: {}
title: Billing - Pay My Bill | Stanford Health Care

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Billing: FAQs | Stanford Health\
  \ Care
- url: https://stanfordhealthcare.org/for-patients-visitors/billing/billing-faqs.html
\
  - source: Stanford Health Care
- domain: stanfordhealthcare.org

## Content
\
  
# Billing: FAQs

## Billing Office Contact

Stanford Health Care lists billing\
  \ office contact information for Stanford Physician and Hospital Billing and University\
  \ Healthcare Alliance. Patients may call 1-800-549-3720 toll free or 650-498-5850\
  \ locally. The listed phone hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
\
  
## Financial Responsibility

The billing FAQ explains that patients should know\
  \ and understand their insurance plan benefits and their responsibility for deductibles,\
  \ co-insurance, or co-payment amounts before a visit. Not all services are covered\
  \ under all insurance contracts. If an insurance plan does not cover a service or\
  \ procedure, the patient may be responsible for full payment of the bill.

Patients\
  \ are advised to contact their insurance company’s Customer Service or Member\
  \ Services department to confirm what the plan covers and what the financial obligation\
  \ may be. The FAQ also notes that patients can verify and update insurance in MyHealth\
  \ under Billing & Insurance.

## Statements, Balances, and EOBs

Stanford Health\
  \ Care states that statements are issued monthly. It explains that an insurance\
  \ provider sends an Explanation of Benefits showing the amount paid, any non-covered\
  \ or denied amounts, and the remaining balance the patient is responsible for paying.\
  \ Patients are advised to review the EOB carefully, compare it to the hospital or\
  \ physician statement, and call the insurance provider or a customer service representative\
  \ with questions.

Outstanding balances are viewable in MyHealth by selecting\
  \ Billing & Insurance and then Billing Summary. Balances are listed under Balance\
  \ Due.

## Payment Due and Online Payment Issues

The FAQ states that payment\
  \ is due when a patient receives a statement in the mail or an email notification\
  \ stating there is a balance due. If a patient is having personal financial difficulty\
  \ and cannot pay the bill, the FAQ directs the patient to call the appropriate billing\
  \ office.

The FAQ also states that Stanford accepts Visa, MasterCard, Discover,\
  \ and American Express credit cards. Credit card information is encrypted during\
  \ transaction transmission, and after a transaction is complete, the patient may\
  \ have the option to save card information for future use.

If a patient is unable\
  \ to view part or all of a bill online, or is having trouble making a payment online,\
  \ the FAQ directs the patient to contact the billing office.
"
metadata: {}
title: 'Billing: FAQs | Stanford Health Care'
url: https://stanfordhealthcare.org/for-patients-visitors/billing/billing-faqs.html

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Help Paying Your Bill | Stanford\
  \ Health Care\n- url: https://stanfordhealthcare.org/for-patients-visitors/financial-assistance.html\n\
  - source: Stanford Health Care\n- domain: stanfordhealthcare.org\n\n## Content\n\
  \n# Help Paying Your Bill\n\nStanford Health Care describes financial options for\
  \ patients who are uninsured or insured but have high medical costs. The page encourages\
  \ patients facing financial hardship to apply to Medi-Cal or explore other government\
  \ programs that may be available. Patients who do not qualify for those programs,\
  \ or who still have substantial financial liability despite insurance, may qualify\
  \ for Stanford Health Care\u2019s financial assistance program.\n\n## Eligibility\
  \ Criteria\n\nThe page states that people with family income at or below 400 percent\
  \ of the federal poverty level are generally eligible for 100 percent assistance.\
  \ It describes eligible groups as uninsured patients with family income not above\
  \ 400 percent of the current federal poverty guidelines and insured patients who\
  \ have income at or below 400 percent of the guidelines and high medical costs.\n\
  \nFor insured patients, high medical costs are described as annual out-of-pocket\
  \ costs at Stanford Health Care exceeding the lesser of 10 percent of current family\
  \ income or family income in the prior 12 months, or annual out-of-pocket expenses\
  \ above 10 percent of family income when the patient provides documentation of medical\
  \ expenses paid by the patient or family during the prior 12 months.\n\n## Apply\
  \ for Financial Assistance\n\nStanford Health Care provides financial assistance\
  \ applications in several languages. The page also provides an online application\
  \ path: patients can log in to a MyHealth account to apply for financial assistance.\
  \ The page links to financial assistance policies, a plain language summary, program\
  \ policy documents, provider lists, debt collection policy, uninsured patient discount\
  \ policy, and related resources.\n\nAdditional information or a statement of charges\
  \ for hospital services may be requested by calling customer service at 1-800-549-3720.\n\
  \n## Financial Assistance Team and Counseling\n\nThe page explains that the Financial\
  \ Assistance team can guide patients through the application process. A video appointment\
  \ can be scheduled to discuss the process overview, eligibility screening, acceptable\
  \ documentation and proof of income, and questions about a financial assistance\
  \ case. To set up an appointment, patients are directed to log in to MyHealth. Video\
  \ visits are provided at no cost.\n\nFinancial counseling is also available to help\
  \ explain coverage and benefits, estimate financial responsibility, identify ways\
  \ to pay for care, identify government coverage options, and address questions about\
  \ insurance coverage and financial assistance. Financial counselors can be reached\
  \ at 844-498-2900, Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.\n\n## Hospital\
  \ Bill Complaint Program\n\nThe page also mentions the Hospital Bill Complaint Program,\
  \ a state program that reviews hospital decisions about whether a patient qualifies\
  \ for help paying a hospital bill.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Help Paying Your Bill | Stanford Health Care
url: https://stanfordhealthcare.org/for-patients-visitors/financial-assistance.html

Here’s what the notice appears to mean:

- **Issuer:** Stanford Health Care  
- **Patient responsibility:** **$318.40**
- **Insurance:** The claim has been processed and an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) was received, so this is currently marked as a **final patient balance**, not an amount still awaiting insurance processing.
- **Due date:** **July 22, 2026** — today is July 15, so it is due in about a week.
- **Payment plan:** You are not currently enrolled in one.
- **Financial assistance:** No application has been started.

### Your options

1. **Review the statement and EOB**
   Compare the Stanford statement with your insurer’s EOB, including the deductible, copay, coinsurance, denied or non-covered amounts, and the services listed. You can also check **MyHealth → Billing & Insurance → Billing Summary**.

2. **Pay the balance**
   Stanford Health Care says you can pay through:
   - **MyHealth** or its **Pay as Guest** billing portal
   - Phone: **1-800-549-3720**
   - Mail: Stanford Health Care, P.O. Box 740715, Los Angeles, CA 90074-0715

3. **Ask about a payment plan**
   If paying $318.40 by the due date is difficult, contact Stanford’s billing office before the due date and ask about payment-plan options.

4. **Apply for financial assistance**
   Stanford says assistance may be available for uninsured patients or insured patients with high medical costs. Its published criteria generally include household income at or below **400% of the federal poverty level**, with additional rules for insured patients. Applications can be started through MyHealth. Financial counselors are available at **844-498-2900**, Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m.

5. **Contact your insurer or Stanford if the amount seems wrong**
   For billing questions, Stanford lists **1-800-549-3720**. Ask them to explain any disputed adjustment or denial and confirm that your insurance information is correct.

**Bottom line:** You do need to address it now because there is a final balance and the due date is approaching, but you do not necessarily have to pay the full amount immediately if you arrange a payment plan or apply for assistance. Avoid using payment links or phone numbers from a suspicious message; access Stanford’s official site or MyHealth directly.

- **Financial assistance:** No application has been started.

1. **Review the statement and EOB**
   Compare the Stanford statement with your insurer’s EOB, including the deductible, copay, coinsurance, denied or non-covered amounts, and the services listed. You can also check **MyHealth → Billing & Insurance → Billing Summary**.

2. **Pay the balance**
   Stanford Health Care says you can pay through:
   - **MyHealth** or its **Pay as Guest** billing portal

   - Mail: Stanford Health Care, P.O. Box 740715, Los Angeles, CA 90074-0715

3. **Ask about a payment plan**
   If paying $318.40 by the due date is difficult, contact Stanford’s billing office before the due date and ask about payment-plan options.

4. **Apply for financial assistance**
   Stanford says assistance may be available for uninsured patients or insured patients with high medical costs. Its published criteria generally include household income at or below **400% of the federal poverty level**, with additional rules for insured patients. Applications can be started through MyHealth. Financial counselors are available at **844-498-2900**, Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m.

5. **Contact your insurer or Stanford if the amount seems wrong**
   For billing questions, Stanford lists **1-800-549-3720**. Ask them to explain any disputed adjustment or denial and confirm that your insurance information is correct.

**Bottom line:** You do need to address it now because there is a final balance and the due date is approaching, but you do not necessarily have to pay the full amount immediately if you arrange a payment plan or apply for assistance.
